segments.text: This video will demonstrate how to view
and manage dependency risks in Sona Cube
for VS Code. You'll need to be in
connected mode to Sonic Cube cloud or
server and have an advanced security
subscription.
Okay, let's head to VS Code. And I'm
already in connected mode with my
project bound to a Sonar Cube cloud
project. If I head to my Sona Cube
panel, I can see all the dependency risk
findings that are synchronized for this
project from SonarCube. I'm shown both
vulnerabilities and prohibited licenses.
Now, here's the best part. I don't need
to go hunting for details. I just click
on the risk and I'm taken straight to
the issue in Sona Cube. Here I get the
full description and also detailed
remediation guidance and crucially I can
see if my code is actually impacted by
this vulnerability.
From here I can either go fix the issue
in my IDE commit my code and rerun the
analysis or in this case I've
investigated and confirmed our code
doesn't use the vulnerable part. So I'll
change it status to safe and add a quick
comment and save. And this updates the
status for the whole team.
This status is then reflected back in
Sona Cube cloud. For more details, check
out the Sonic Cube documentation linked
below. Happy coding.

fullText: This video will demonstrate how to view and manage dependency risks in Sona Cube for VS Code. You'll need to be in connected mode to Sonic Cube cloud or server and have an advanced security subscription. Okay, let's head to VS Code. And I'm already in connected mode with my project bound to a Sonar Cube cloud project. If I head to my Sona Cube panel, I can see all the dependency risk findings that are synchronized for this project from SonarCube. I'm shown both vulnerabilities and prohibited licenses. Now, here's the best part. I don't need to go hunting for details. I just click on the risk and I'm taken straight to the issue in Sona Cube. Here I get the full description and also detailed remediation guidance and crucially I can see if my code is actually impacted by this vulnerability. From here I can either go fix the issue in my IDE commit my code and rerun the analysis or in this case I've investigated and confirmed our code doesn't use the vulnerable part. So I'll change it status to safe and add a quick comment and save. And this updates the status for the whole team. This status is then reflected back in Sona Cube cloud. For more details, check out the Sonic Cube documentation linked below. Happy coding.
